summary: Energetic Truckin' and solid Terrapin amid uneven set
blurb: The show bursts with a high‑octane Truckin' and a tight Terrapin Station, while vocal delivery is hit‑or‑miss. Highlights include Bird Song, Hell In A Bucket, Man Smart/Woman Smarter and a rousing Johnny B. Goode encore.
review: Feb 19 1985 at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center captures the Dead riding the post‑bust excitement of a Chinese New Year gig. The second set shines with a crowd‑charged Truckin' that segues into a crisp Terrapin Station, followed by an upbeat Man Smart, Woman Smarter that sparked a spontaneous Iko jam. Bird Song and Hell In A Bucket deliver the usual fire, and the encore’s Johnny B. Goode leaves the audience buzzing. Jerry’s guitar stays on point throughout, though his vocals wobble in places, while Brent’s keys add lively color. Phil’s bass hits a few “bombing” moments but locks in during the high‑energy numbers. Bob peppers the set with strong vocal bursts and leads the Hell In A Bucket fireworks. The percussion duo of Mickey and Bill adds a unique Dragon Dance flavor that only this night could provide. Overall, the performance is solid if uneven – some songs feel rushed or sloppy, yet the standout moments make it worth a listen for fans seeking the era’s raw energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings (soundboard and audience) praise the same high‑energy songs – Truckin', Terrapin Station, Bird Song, Hell In A Bucket – while only one source notes vocal sloppiness. Positive remarks outweigh the singular negative note, yielding a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp audience tape captures the concert’s high energy, crowd interaction and unique percussion segment with no major glitches, offering a more vivid listening experience than the soundboard version.

key_highlights: Truckin' ignites the crowd with a memorable jam
Terrapin Station delivers a solid, melodic performance
Man Smart, Woman Smarter leads into an improvised Iko jam
Johnny B. Goode encore caps the night on a high note

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar work stays sharp and energetic throughout; vocals are uneven, described as croaky on the soundboard but spirited on the audience tape.
band_performance.Phil: Bass occasionally 'bombs' but launches strong lines in Hell In A Bucket, keeping the low end solid during the high‑energy numbers.
band_performance.Bob: Adds vocal fireworks on Hell In A Bucket and drives the Iko jam; occasional vocal lines noted elsewhere.
band_performance.Brent: Lively keyboard playing that lifts the set, especially evident in the Women medley and overall groove.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums contribute a unique Dragon Dance percussion segment that adds cultural flavor to the show.
band_performance.Billy: Provides steady rhythmic support alongside Mickey; together they enhance the percussive texture of the second set.
